sections.current_situation:
The Civic Arts Grant award amounts (Attachment 1), as approved by the Civic Arts
Commission, include two annual grant categories (FY26 Individual Artist Projects and
FY26 Community Festivals), and one biennial grant category (FY26/27 General
Operating Support). Awards in the annual Civic Arts Grant categories for FY26 totaled
$50,000 for ten (10) Individual Artist Projects and $158,500 for twenty-nine (29)
Community Festivals, and $449,714 for seventy-two (72) organizations for General
Operating Support.
The Civic Arts program received a total of 202 grant applications in the recent cycle.
Similar to the previous grant cycle, there was high demand for Individual Artist Projects
grants, with a total of 49 applications. The high volume of applications was received
even with strategic changes to this grant category. In order to attempt funding a higher
percentage of applicants, the Individual Artist Projects grant piloted a two-year split
among artistic disciplines; artists could apply for projects in film and new media,
multidisciplinary/interdisciplinary, social practice, and visual arts disciplines in FY26, and
dance, folk and traditional arts, literary arts, music, and theater projects in FY27. Hoping
to reduce the applicant pool by 50% with this strategy, however, it only reduced the
applicant pool by approximately 15%.
The Community Festivals category received 57 applications, a 33% increase from the
last cycle which had 43 applications. The City has consistently included $158,315 in its
budget for this program category. In previous years, the Council has provided additional
funds to bring the total allocation up to $200,000, however, for FY26, no additional
funds were appropriated to the Community Festivals grant category due to the City’s

sections.background:
The Civic Arts FY26 grant application period started January 6, 2025 and had staggered
application deadlines for each grant category (see table below):
FY25 Civic Arts Grant Program Category Grant Application Deadline
Individual Artist Projects February 19, 2025
Arts Programs February 26, 2025
Community Festivals March 5, 2025
General Operating Support March 12, 2025
In January 2025, at the beginning of the application period, Civic Arts staff held four
grant webinars (one workshop for each grant category) to inform applicants about
eligibility, application requirements, and to provide instruction on using the online grants
submittal and management system. All webinars were held virtually using Zoom
Page 2
Page 3 of 9
FY26 Civic Arts Grant Awards INFORMATION CALENDAR
October 14, 2025
Webinar. Recordings of the webinars were also made available on the Civic Arts
website for applicants’ reference.
To evaluate the grant applications, Civic Arts staff selected panelists from a Civic Arts
Commission-approved pool of potential panelists. The pool consists of individuals from
the greater Bay Area who have substantial backgrounds in arts and culture and a
demonstrated commitment to cultural equity. All panelists participated in an orientation
and training for their panel service. Between May 13 and June 4, 2025, staff conducted
five grant review panel meetings via Zoom webinar. Panelists independently evaluated
and scored applications relative to the review criteria prior to the panel meetings and
discussed their evaluations with fellow panelists during the meetings.
At the conclusion of the grant review process, the compiled panel scores were
presented to the Civic Arts Commission's Grants Subcommittee. The Subcommittee
determined award recommendations based on application scores, the grant program
guidelines, funding criteria, and available funding. The Grants Subcommittee’s award
amount recommendations were then forwarded to the full Civic Arts Commission for
approval on

sections.preamble:
Page 1 of 9
Office of the City Manager
INFORMATION CALENDAR
October 14, 2025
To: Honorable Mayor and Members of the City Council
From: Paul Buddenhagen, City Manager
Submitted by: Eleanor Hollander, Economic Development Manager
Subject: Civic Arts Grant Awards: Fiscal Year 2026
INTRODUCTION
This report summarizes the Civic Arts Grant awards for the budget appropriated for
Fiscal Year 2026 (FY26). It includes FY26 Civic Arts Grant categories of Individual Artist
Projects (10 awards), Community Festivals (29 awards), and Arts Organization General
Operating Support (72 awards) for a total of 91 awards totaling $658,214.
